rules.title: Forbid server-only imports in `'use client'` files (R12)
Server Action files must start with `'use server'` directive (R20)

examples.good:
'use server';
export async function action() { return 1; }

rulesMd:
<!-- from preset-next-15-canonical/eslint-rules/no-server-imports-in-client.ts -->
## G1 — Forbid server-only imports in `'use client'` files (R12)

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** ESLint `rules-as-tests/no-server-imports-in-client` (preset plugin rule)  
**Why:** `'use client'` modules execute in the browser. Server-only modules (`node:fs`, `node:crypto`, `node:path`, infrastructure adapters, `config/env`) crash at runtime or break the bundler. Pass server data through props from a parent Server Component instead.

<!-- from preset-next-15-canonical/eslint-rules/require-form-safe-parse.ts -->
## G2 — Validate `FormData` parameters via `.safeParse(...)` (R14)

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** ESLint `rules-as-tests/require-form-safe-parse` (preset plugin rule)  
**Why:** Server Actions and form handlers that accept `FormData` are the trust boundary; unvalidated `formData.get('name')` is the most common Server Action footgun. Validate via a Zod schema's `.safeParse(Object.fromEntries(formData))` before reading values.

<!-- from preset-next-15-canonical/eslint-rules/require-use-server-directive.ts -->
## G3 — Server Action files must start with `'use server'` directive (R20)

**Stack:** react-next  
**Check:** ESLint `rules-as-tests/require-use-server-directive` (preset plugin rule)  
**Why:** Without `'use server'` as the first program statement, Next compiles `async function` exports as regular module code — server logic can leak into client bundles or fail to register as a Server Action. The directive must be the very first statement (no imports or side-effects before it).
